steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content: The sentence has two independent clauses joined at the blank. Left side: "Since 1989, Indianapolis's Eiteljorg Museum has celebrated the work of groundbreaking contemporary Native [artists]" — a complete sentence. Right side: "in 2015, it awarded Wilson one of its prestigious artist fellowships" — also a complete sentence. Rule: two independent clauses must be separated by a semicolon (or a period), not a comma alone.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
Choice A — artists and — "and" is a coordinating conjunction, but without a comma before it, this creates a run-on. Reconstruct: "...Native artists and in 2015, it awarded..." — the comma placement is wrong and the clause boundary is blurred. Eliminated.

Choice B — artists; — a semicolon correctly separates the two independent clauses. Reconstruct: "...Native artists; in 2015, it awarded Wilson..." — grammatically complete on both sides. Correct.

Choice C — artists, — a comma alone between two independent clauses is a comma splice. Reconstruct: "...Native artists, in 2015, it awarded Wilson..." — two full sentences joined by only a comma. Eliminated.

Choice D — artists, thus — "thus" is a conjunctive adverb; it needs a semicolon before it and a comma after it ("artists; thus, in 2015..."). A comma before "thus" creates a comma splice. Eliminated.
